quickAnswer: For creating multiple images that feel visually consistent, start with Midjourney because it is the closest fit for turning product photos into edited images that match brand style. Choose Krea when brand colors matters more. Avoid Midjourney when you need a free-only workflow; in that case Leonardo AI is the better fallback.

faqs.question: What input do I need before using AI for creating multiple images that feel visually consistent?
faqs.answer: Start with product photos, brand colors, reference images and decide that the output must be edited images that match brand style. That keeps Midjourney focused on the actual job instead of drifting into a generic answer.

faqs.question: Which tool should I start with for edited images that match brand style?
faqs.answer: Start with Midjourney when the main input is product photos. Compare Krea when brand colors matters more, and keep Leonardo AI for review or fallback work.

faqs.question: What should I check before I use the result?
faqs.answer: Check the result against product photos, especially letting the background style drift. Do not use the output until it still matches edited images that match brand style.

faqs.question: Can I finish this workflow on a phone, or should I use a computer?

finalVerdict: For visual creators, start by testing Midjourney on one realistic assignment: creating multiple images that feel visually consistent.
